standards.subject: General Science

understandings: Atoms are the fundamental building blocks of matter with specific subatomic structures.
The periodic table organizes elements by properties that emerge from electron configuration.
essentialQuestions: How does the structure of an atom determine its chemical behavior?
Why is the periodic table organized the way it is?

understandings: Chemical bonds form to achieve more stable electron configurations.
The type of bonding determines the physical properties of a substance.
essentialQuestions: Why do atoms bond with each other?
How does bonding type affect macroscopic properties?

activities: Properties of ionic vs covalent compounds lab

understandings: Cells are the basic unit of life with specialized organelles.
essentialQuestions: How do cell structures support life functions?
How do cells regulate what enters and exits?

understandings: DNA structure determines protein synthesis and inheritance patterns.
Gene expression is regulated by environmental and cellular factors.
essentialQuestions: How does DNA encode information for life?

understandings: Scientific inquiry follows systematic processes of observation, hypothesis, and experimentation.
Variables must be controlled to draw valid conclusions.
essentialQuestions: How do scientists design reliable experiments?
What makes evidence valid and trustworthy?

understandings: Matter can change form but is conserved in chemical and physical changes.
Energy flows and transforms but is conserved in closed systems.
essentialQuestions: How can we tell if a chemical change has occurred?
Where does energy go during a transformation?

assessments: Physical vs chemical changes lab practical

integrations.name: Google Classroom
integrations.description: Sync roster and assignments with Google Classroom.

integrations.name: Google Slides
integrations.description: Embed slide presentations directly into learning map nodes.

integrations.name: Canvas LMS
integrations.description: Import grades and export progress data to Canvas.

teachingMethods.title: Welcome to Hex-Based Learning
teachingMethods.content: Hex-based learning maps transform traditional curriculum into visual, interactive pathways. Students navigate hexagonal nodes, each representing a lesson, activity, or assessment, building understanding as they progress.

teachingMethods.title: Setting Up Your First Map
teachingMethods.content: Start by identifying the key concepts in your unit. Create hexes for each concept, then connect them to show prerequisite relationships. Use different hex types (lesson, activity, assessment) to vary the learning experience.

teachingMethods.title: The Hex Learning Cycle
teachingMethods.content: Each hex follows a cycle: Introduce (lesson) → Practice (activity) → Assess (checkpoint). This ensures students build, apply, and demonstrate knowledge at every stage.

teachingMethods.title: Differentiation Through Pathways
teachingMethods.content: Create multiple paths through the map to differentiate instruction. Advanced students can take shortcut paths while struggling students follow scaffolded routes with additional support hexes.

teachingMethods.title: Interactive Map Builder Demo
teachingMethods.content: Try adding hexes, creating connections, and editing node properties. The map builder supports drag-and-drop positioning, connection drawing, and real-time collaboration.

teachingMethods.title: Configuring Student Progress Tracking
teachingMethods.content: Set completion requirements for each hex: automatic (view-based), manual (student marks complete), or scored (must achieve minimum score). Configure approval requirements for teacher review.

teachingMethods.title: Integrating with Google Classroom
teachingMethods.content: Connect your Google Classroom account to automatically import student rosters and sync assignment data. Map completions can be pushed as grades to Classroom.

teachingMethods.title: Linear Progression Template
teachingMethods.content: A simple sequential path: Introduction → Guided Practice → Independent Practice → Assessment → Reflection. Best for topics with clear prerequisite chains.

teachingMethods.title: Branching Exploration Template
teachingMethods.content: A central hub with branching paths students choose between. Converges at a final assessment. Ideal for units where multiple topics can be studied in any order.

teachingMethods.title: Spiral Review Template
teachingMethods.content: Concepts revisit earlier material at increasing depth. Includes periodic review checkpoints that spiral back to reinforce prior learning.

ealStrategies.title: Word Wall Integration
ealStrategies.description: Create visual word walls within hex maps that students can reference as they navigate lessons.
steps: Identify key vocabulary for each hex

ealStrategies.title: Sentence Frame Scaffolding
ealStrategies.description: Embed sentence frames in activity hexes to help EAL students structure their responses.
steps: Identify writing tasks in the map
Create tiered sentence frames by WIDA level
Gradually reduce scaffolding in later hexes

ealStrategies.title: Visual Anchor Charts
ealStrategies.description: Attach visual anchor charts to lesson hexes showing key concepts through images and diagrams.
steps: Create anchor charts for abstract concepts

ealStrategies.title: Collaborative Discussion Protocols
ealStrategies.description: Structure group discussion hexes with protocols that support language development.

ealStrategies.title: Modified Assessment Strategies
ealStrategies.description: Create alternative assessment hexes that allow EAL students to demonstrate knowledge through multiple modalities.
steps: Offer visual/oral alternatives to written tests
Allow extended time on assessment hexes
Use rubrics that separate content from language

ealStrategies.title: Content-Language Objective Pairing
ealStrategies.description: Each hex includes both a content objective and a language objective, making language development explicit.
steps: Write content objectives for each hex
Add language objectives aligned to WIDA Can-Do descriptors
Design activities that practice both objectives
Assess both content and language growth

ealStrategies.title: Graphic Organizer Library
ealStrategies.description: Provide graphic organizers as downloadable resources within resource hexes to support comprehension.
steps: Select appropriate organizers for each topic
Create simplified versions for lower WIDA levels

ealStrategies.title: Peer Translation Support
ealStrategies.description: Enable collaborative hexes where multilingual students can support each other with translations and explanations.
steps: Identify bilingual students in each group

flashcards.question: What are the three subatomic particles and their charges?
What determines the identity of an element?
What is the Aufbau principle?
flashcards.answer: Electrons fill orbitals from lowest to highest energy levels.

flashcards.question: Name the 5 main types of chemical reactions.
flashcards.answer: Synthesis, Decomposition, Single Replacement, Double Replacement, Combustion

flashcards.question: What is the general form of a synthesis reaction?
What is the general form of a decomposition reaction?
What must be conserved in a balanced chemical equation?
flashcards.answer: The number of atoms of each element on both sides (Law of Conservation of Mass)

flashcards.question: Why can carbon form so many compounds?
flashcards.answer: Carbon has 4 valence electrons, allowing it to form 4 covalent bonds in many configurations.

flashcards.question: What is the general formula for alkanes?
What causes the different colors in a flame test?
flashcards.answer: Excited electrons returning to ground state emit photons at specific wavelengths.

flashcards.question: What are isotopes?
flashcards.answer: Atoms of the same element with different numbers of neutrons.

flashcards.question: What determines if a single replacement reaction will occur?
flashcards.answer: The activity series — more reactive metals displace less reactive ones.

flashcards.question: What are the products of complete combustion of a hydrocarbon?
What makes alkenes more reactive than alkanes?
flashcards.answer: The C=C double bond can undergo addition reactions, breaking the pi bond.
